forecaster_note: Fun clean surf at the south exposures this morning — a bit of bump later but still alright. Mid-period S swell filtering in, hopefully chest to head high at the S magnets; other breaks stay quite small. Clean offshore W winds through the morning, then light–moderate S'ly. INCOMING: solid winter swell — Sunday solid but breezy SW–S; Tue/Wed the standout clean overhead days. — Hugh McDowell (Surfline)

wind.summary: Light WNW offshores through the morning, trending light–moderate S'ly with some bump after midday

shark.summary: Busy day yesterday (Fri): Dee Why closed twice — shark sighted 8:48am (beach closed, reopened 11:25am), drone sighting 12:41pm (evacuated again), then a 3.38m tiger shark tagged & released off the Dee Why SMART drumline at 1:20pm. North Curl Curl evacuated 1:38pm and South Narrabeen twice (12:32pm, 2:36pm). No detections overnight — check surf club status before paddling DY or Curly.

days.label: Today
days.note: Your window lines up: clean WNW offshores all morning and the 1.2m high at 11:12am sits right in your 10–12 slot. The 12s swell is dead S (182°) — it underperforms at Dee Why and lights up the S magnets, so Long Reef is the call. S'ly bump arrives after midday. Big shark day yesterday at DY/Curly — scan the lineup and check flags first.

dee-why.window: Backup only — dead S 182° wraps badly here. If you stay local, surf the 9–11:30am glass into the high.
alerts: Closed twice yesterday: sightings 8:48am & 12:41pm, 3.38m tiger tagged off the drumline 1:20pm — check flags before paddling.

tip.label: Skip DY — swell angle wrong
tip.text: 106kJ but only 36/100 consistency and the energy is all dead-S 12s, the one angle that wraps badly into this bank. Same swell is hitting Long Reef square. Don't burn the morning waiting on sets here.

long-reef.window: Today's call: 9–11:30am into the 11:11am high, clean WNW offshore before the S'ly. Best energy on the stretch.

tip.label: Today's call — 166kJ, S magnet
tip.text: The south side soaks up the dead-S 12s that Dee Why wastes — 166kJ and fair-to-good, chest to overhead on sets. Frontside on the rights off the reef. Get it before the S'ly bumps it after midday.

curl-curl.window: Solid plan B — most consistent on the stretch (57/100). Morning into the high tide.
alerts: North Curl Curl evacuated 1:38pm yesterday after a drone shark sighting.

tip.label: Plan B — 57/100 consistency
tip.text: Curly eats S swell: 138kJ and the best waves-per-hour number on the coast today. Waist–chest peaks, rights off the south end for your frontside. Shark drone cleared the water here yesterday arvo — stay aware.

manly.window: Swell shadow on a dead-S — small and soft. Save it for Sunday's shelter.

tip.label: Pass — 89kJ shadow
tip.text: North-facing Manly barely sees 182° S swell. 89kJ of thigh-high. Its day is tomorrow when the solid S arrives and this corner is the sheltered option.

days.label: Tomorrow
days.note: Solid winter swell arrives — Dee Why 4–5ft, Long Reef 6–8ft — but breezy-to-strong SW–S winds limit options. Dawn patrol before it blows, or hunt the sheltered southern corners. Manly's protection makes it the smart mid-morning bet.

dee-why.window: Dawn before the SW–S wind builds; your fav size but wind-affected — the Point could handle it if the angle has any E in it.

long-reef.window: Serious size for the outer reefs — experienced crew only, early, before the S'ly gets strong.

curl-curl.window: Big and raw with S wind on it — south corner early or leave it.

manly.window: The S-wind shelter: wrapping swell in the protected corner while everywhere else is blown. Best Sunday bet for your 10–12 window.

days.label: Weekend
days.note: Remaining weekend = Sunday: solid swell, breezy SW–S winds. Sheltered corners (Manly) or dawn only. The real prize is Tue–Wed — clean overhead-plus mornings as the swell holds and winds back off.

dee-why.window: Sun dawn patrol only — wind-limited. Tue/Wed look like the quality DY days.

long-reef.window: Sun: XL and wind-affected. Watch Tue for OH–2xOH clean.

curl-curl.window: Sun: raw S-swell size; south corner at dawn or skip.

manly.window: Sun: the sheltered call — fun wrapping waist–shoulder waves while the open beaches are blown out.
